ingredients.item:
Mì Quảng noodles are a specialty from the Quang Nam Province and Da Nang city in Central Vietnam, which also happens to be where I'm from. This half-noodle, half-salad dish is the ultimate comfort food. Unlike other noodle soups, fresh greens are part of this dish, not optional add-ins. Although we use a lot less of it for each serving, the broth should also be saltier than a normal soup, yet not as salty as a sauce. Every cook has their own variation of mì Quảng. In fact, this is my mom's recipe, who I believe makes the best mì Quảng in the world! Mì Quảng is known for its wide, vibrant yellow turmeric noodles; however, you can use flat, wide white rice noodles (sợi mì Quảng).
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD8 to 10 servings
6 cups (1.5 L) water
12½ ounces (350 g) pumpkin, peeled and cut into cubes
9 ounces (250 g) chayote or kohlrabi, peeled and half cubed and half diced
ingredients.original:
Mì Quảng noodles are a specialty from the Quang Nam Province and Da Nang city in Central Vietnam, which also happens to be where I'm from. This half-noodle, half-salad dish is the ultimate comfort food. Unlike other noodle soups, fresh greens are part of this dish, not optional add-ins. Although we use a lot less of it for each serving, the broth should also be saltier than a normal soup, yet not as salty as a sauce. Every cook has their own variation of mì Quảng. In fact, this is my mom's recipe, who I believe makes the best mì Quảng in the world! Mì Quảng is known for its wide, vibrant yellow turmeric noodles; however, you can use flat, wide white rice noodles (sợi mì Quảng).
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD8 to 10 servings
6 cups (1.5 L) water
12½ ounces (350 g) pumpkin, peeled and cut into cubes
9 ounces (250 g) chayote or kohlrabi, peeled and half cubed and half diced

ingredients.item: 7 ounces (200 g) taro, diced
ingredients.original: 7 ounces (200 g) taro, diced

ingredients.item: 2½ teaspoons salt, divided
ingredients.original: 2½ teaspoons salt, divided

ingredients.item: ½ cup (120 ml) cold-pressed peanut or vegetable oil, divided
ingredients.original: ½ cup (120 ml) cold-pressed peanut or vegetable oil, divided

ingredients.item: 1 block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) thick steaks
ingredients.original: 1 block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) thick steaks

ingredients.item: 2 finger-size pieces fresh turmeric (see Note), peeled and roughly chopped
ingredients.original: 2 finger-size pieces fresh turmeric (see Note), peeled and roughly chopped

ingredients.item: 2 ounces (56 g) leek (white and green parts separated), roughly chopped, divided
ingredients.original: 2 ounces (56 g) leek (white and green parts separated), roughly chopped, divided

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) mushrooms (straw, shiitake, or similar), cut into bite-size pieces
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) mushrooms (straw, shiitake, or similar), cut into bite-size pieces

ingredients.item: 4¼ ounces (120 g) store-bought or homemade seitan, sliced
ingredients.original: 4¼ ounces (120 g) store-bought or homemade seitan, sliced

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon sugar
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon sugar

ingredients.item: 16 ounces (454 g) dried flat rice noodles or 2¼ pounds (1 kg) fresh mì Quảng or flat rice noodles
ingredients.original: 16 ounces (454 g) dried flat rice noodles or 2¼ pounds (1 kg) fresh mì Quảng or flat rice noodles

ingredients.item: 10½ ounces (300 g) leaf or iceberg lettuce, shredded or torn
ingredients.original: 10½ ounces (300 g) leaf or iceberg lettuce, shredded or torn

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) fresh green herbs (such as mint, perilla, cilantro, and/or Asian basil leaves), chopped
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) fresh green herbs (such as mint, perilla, cilantro, and/or Asian basil leaves), chopped

ingredients.item: 2 sesame rice crackers
ingredients.original: 2 sesame rice crackers

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) cilantro, chopped, for garnish
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) cilantro, chopped, for garnish

ingredients.item: ½ cup (72 g) crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish
ingredients.original: ½ cup (72 g) crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish

ingredients.item: 2 limes, cut into wedges, for serving
ingredients.original: 2 limes, cut into wedges, for serving

ingredients.item: 5 whole green serrano chili peppers. for serving
ingredients.original: 5 whole green serrano chili peppers. for serving

ingredients.item: 3½ ounces (100 g) banana blossom, prepped and shredded
ingredients.original: 3½ ounces (100 g) banana blossom, prepped and shredded

ingredients.item: 1. Add the water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add pumpkin and chayote cubes, cook until fork-tender (about 15 minutes), and then remove from heat. Purée the mixture with a hand blender. Add diced chayote and taro to saucepan with 1 teaspoon of the salt and cook over medium heat until fork-tender (about 7 minutes).
ingredients.original: 1. Add the water to a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add pumpkin and chayote cubes, cook until fork-tender (about 15 minutes), and then remove from heat. Purée the mixture with a hand blender. Add diced chayote and taro to saucepan with 1 teaspoon of the salt and cook over medium heat until fork-tender (about 7 minutes).

ingredients.item: 2. Heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of the oil in a small skillet over medium-low heat. When hot, add tofu steaks and cook until golden brown on all sides (10 to 15 minutes). Transfer to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 2. Heat ¼ cup (60 ml) of the oil in a small skillet over medium-low heat. When hot, add tofu steaks and cook until golden brown on all sides (10 to 15 minutes). Transfer to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 3. If using fresh turmeric, place it in a resealable plastic bag and crush it with a pestle (avoid using the mortar, as the turmeric may stain it and/or your hands) or meat tenderizer.
ingredients.original: 3. If using fresh turmeric, place it in a resealable plastic bag and crush it with a pestle (avoid using the mortar, as the turmeric may stain it and/or your hands) or meat tenderizer.

ingredients.item: 4. In a mortar and pestle, finely crush the white part of the leek with ½ teaspoon of the salt.
ingredients.original: 4. In a mortar and pestle, finely crush the white part of the leek with ½ teaspoon of the salt.

ingredients.item: 5. In a medium skillet, heat the remaining ¼ cup (60 ml) oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until golden (about 2 minutes). If using fresh turmeric, add and quickly cook it for about 10 seconds.
ingredients.original: 5. In a medium skillet, heat the remaining ¼ cup (60 ml) oil over medium-low heat. When hot, add leek and cook and stir until golden (about 2 minutes). If using fresh turmeric, add and quickly cook it for about 10 seconds.

ingredients.item: 6. Add mushrooms, tofu, and seitan to the skillet. Season with turmeric powder (if using), remaining 1 teaspoon salt, stock powder, and sugar. Toss well, cook over low heat until mushrooms are tender (2 to 3 minutes), and then turn off heat. Add green part of leek and ground pepper.
ingredients.original: 6. Add mushrooms, tofu, and seitan to the skillet. Season with turmeric powder (if using), remaining 1 teaspoon salt, stock powder, and sugar. Toss well, cook over low heat until mushrooms are tender (2 to 3 minutes), and then turn off heat. Add green part of leek and ground pepper.

ingredients.item: 7. Follow the directions on the package to cook the dried flat rice noodles. If using fresh noodles, quickly blanch them in hot water if they have been refrigerated.
ingredients.original: 7. Follow the directions on the package to cook the dried flat rice noodles. If using fresh noodles, quickly blanch them in hot water if they have been refrigerated.

ingredients.item: 8. Fill serving bowls halfway with noodles, and then ladle soup over the top until it barely covers them. Add some lettuce and herbs to each bowl. Break a rice cracker into small pieces and add to bowl. Garnish with cilantro, crushed peanuts, and a full piece of sesame rice cracker.
ingredients.original: 8. Fill serving bowls halfway with noodles, and then ladle soup over the top until it barely covers them. Add some lettuce and herbs to each bowl. Break a rice cracker into small pieces and add to bowl. Garnish with cilantro, crushed peanuts, and a full piece of sesame rice cracker.

ingredients.item: 9. Squeeze lime wedges over the top of the bowls and mix well. Serve with serrano chili peppers, banana blossom, and more lettuce and herbs.
ingredients.original: 9. Squeeze lime wedges over the top of the bowls and mix well. Serve with serrano chili peppers, banana blossom, and more lettuce and herbs.
